clusters.title: 6,500 Volunteers Construct 10,000 Beds for Needy Children
clusters.summary: In a remarkable display of community spirit, 6,500 volunteers gathered at the Charlotte Convention Center in North Carolina to build over 10,000 beds for children in need within a 24-hour period. The initiative, supported by Lowe's employees and various local organizations, aims to provide comfort and stability to children who lack proper sleeping arrangements. This event highlights the power of collective action in addressing child welfare issues.

items.title: 6,500 Volunteers Build 10,000 Beds For Children Who Don’t Have One Of Their Own
6,500 Volunteers Build 10,000 Beds in 24 Hours for Kids Who Don’t Have One of Their Own (WATCH)
items.description:
6,500 volunteers worked around the clock to build over 10,000 beds in just 24 hours for kids who don’t have one of their own. items.title: 250 Millionth Tree Planted! Eco-Loving Search Engine Ecosia Hits Milestone For Earth Day
items.description:
Reaching a stellar milestone just before Earth Day, Ecosia, the nonprofit search engine, is celebrating 250 million trees planted worldwide, becoming the world’s largest planter of native trees. Since its founding in 2009, Ecosia has built the world’s largest network of local reforestation operations, numbering more than 200,000 tree planters and 125 organizations worldwide. Users’ […]

clusters.title: After Hiker Falls From Cliff, Dog is Left Behind Shivering Until Pilot Crowdfunds for Rescue Flight to Reunite Them
clusters.summary: Jessica Johnston was lucky to have survived after plummeting from the top of a 180-foot waterfall while hiking in the mountains. But when she lost her footing—she also lost her dog. They were enjoying one of the world’s richest landscapes, New Zealand’s South Island—with its rainforests, glaciers, fjords, and crystal-clear lakes.

clusters.title: When a Simple Act of Kindness Got Complicated
clusters.summary: When Nate Walls' barbecue catering company collapsed with the pandemic, he took what remained in his bank account and started knocking on doors, delivering free meals to anyone who needed it in Fayetteville, Arkansas. In one trailer park, a man opened the door aggressively and refused the food with a racist slur. Nate could have walked away and never returned, "but there are 59 other people in that trailer park that need to eat." So he kept knocking and kept offering a plate.

items.title: When a Simple Act of Kindness Got Complicated
items.description: When Nate Walls' barbecue catering company collapsed with the pandemic, he took what remained in his bank account and started knocking on doors, delivering free meals to anyone who needed it in Fayetteville, Arkansas. In one trailer park, a man opened the door aggressively and refused the food with a racist slur. Nate could have walked away and never returned, "but there are 59 other people in that trailer park that need to eat." So he kept knocking and kept offering a plate. One day, the man accepted a plate without words. Gradually, hostility from this man softened into conversation about sports, bills, family, and the ways life had been hard for both of them. After awhile, he genuinely apologized to Nate for his earlier offensive behavior. "Well, I made it a point to keep returning to that trailer park," described Nate. Sometimes, transformation happens one meal and one returned knock at a time.
